title: Policies for Information Security
text: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel, and reviewed at planned intervals.

title: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
text: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization's needs.

title: Threat Intelligence
text: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence.

title: Information Security in Project Management
text: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

title: Inventory of Information and Other Associated Assets
text: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

title: Acceptable Use of Information and Other Associated Assets
text: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented, and implemented.

title: Access Control
text: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

title: Identity Management
text: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

title: Authentication Information
text: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling.

title: Access Rights
text: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified, and removed in accordance with the access control policy.

title: Information Security in Supplier Relationships
text: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of suppliers' products or services.

title: Information Security for Use of Cloud Services
text: Processes for acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.

title: Information Security Incident Management Planning
text: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing, and communicating incident management processes, roles, and responsibilities.

title: Information Security During Disruption
text: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

title: ICT Readiness for Business Continuity
text: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained, and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

title: Legal, Statutory, Regulatory and Contractual Requirements
text: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security shall be identified, documented, and kept up to date.

title: Privacy and Protection of PII
text: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of personally identifiable information according to applicable requirements.

title: Screening
text: Background verification checks on candidates shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis, taking into consideration applicable laws and proportional to business requirements.

title: Terms and Conditions of Employment
text: Employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.

title: Information Security Awareness, Education and Training
text: Personnel and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education, and training and regular updates of policies relevant to their job function.

title: Responsibilities After Termination or Change of Employment
text: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced, and communicated.

title: Physical Security Perimeters
text: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

title: Physical Entry
text: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

title: Physical Security Monitoring
text: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

title: User Endpoint Devices
text: Information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

title: Privileged Access Rights
text: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

title: Secure Authentication
text: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the access control policy, including multi-factor authentication where appropriate.

title: Protection Against Malware
text: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

title: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
text: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the organization's exposure evaluated, and appropriate measures taken.

title: Configuration Management
text: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services, and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed.

title: Data Leakage Prevention
text: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks, and any other devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information.

title: Information Backup
text: Backup copies of information, software, and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed backup policy.

title: Logging
text: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults, and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected, and analyzed.

title: Monitoring Activities
text: Networks, systems, and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behavior and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

title: Networks Security
text: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed, and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

title: Web Filtering
text: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

title: Use of Cryptography
text: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

title: Secure Development Life Cycle
text: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

title: Secure Coding
text: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

title: Change Management
text: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.
